overrides.override_summary: CA Franchise Relations Act §20025 requires 180-day written notice for non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Licensee gets 6 months notice before non-renewal, longer than default agreement provides

overrides.override_summary: CA Civil Code §1671: certain liquidated damages clauses unenforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: $100K minimum liquidated damages may not be enforceable in CA

overrides.override_summary: CA Corp Code §31512 voids waiver of rights under Franchise Investment Law; Bus & Prof Code §20010 voids waiver under Franchise Relations Act
overrides.why_it_matters: General release on renewal/transfer cannot waive state franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire/acknowledgment can waive franchise law claims or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects licensee's right to rely on franchisor representations

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs interpretation and enforcement of License Agreement for IL licensees
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides NY choice of law — IL law controls for IL-based licensees

overrides.override_summary: Forum selection clause designating Orange County CA is VOID for IL causes of action
overrides.why_it_matters: IL licensees can sue in Illinois, not forced to go to California

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire/acknowledgment can waive franchise law claims or disclaim reliance
overrides.why_it_matters: Standard anti-waiver protection

overrides.override_summary: Indiana Code §23-2-2.7-1(10): unlawful to limit litigation in any manner
overrides.why_it_matters: IN licensees cannot be forced to litigate in CA

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire/acknowledgment can waive franchise law claims or disclaim reliance
overrides.why_it_matters: Standard anti-waiver protection

overrides.override_summary: MI prohibits certain unfair franchise provisions; such provisions are void
overrides.why_it_matters: Any unfair provisions in franchise documents are automatically void

overrides.override_summary: 90-day notice of termination with 60-day cure required
overrides.why_it_matters: More protection than default 30-day cure — licensee gets 90 days notice and 60 days to fix

overrides.override_summary: 180-day notice required for non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: 6 months advance notice before license non-renewal

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damage provisions are VOID in Minnesota
overrides.why_it_matters: $100K+ liquidated damages clause is completely unenforceable in MN

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor cannot require litigation outside Minnesota (Minn. Stat. §80c.21, Rule 2860.4400j)
overrides.why_it_matters: MN licensees cannot be forced to litigate in CA

overrides.override_summary: General release provisions prohibited (MN Rule 2860.4400D)
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot be required to sign general release on renewal/transfer

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must indemnify MN licensees against third-party trademark infringement claims
overrides.why_it_matters: If sued for trademark infringement due to using Taco Bell marks, franchisor covers costs

overrides.override_summary: Licensee may terminate on any grounds available by law (replaces 'termination by licensee = default' provision)
overrides.why_it_matters: NY licensees can exit without it automatically being treated as a breach with $100K damages

overrides.override_summary: NY GBL Article 33 rights preserved; §687(4) and §687(5) non-waiver provisions apply
overrides.why_it_matters: Renewal/transfer conditions cannot override NY franchise law protections

overrides.override_summary: Choice of law does not waive NY General Business Law Article 33 rights
overrides.why_it_matters: Even with NY governing law, NY-specific franchise protections remain

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire/acknowledgment can waive franchise law claims or disclaim reliance
overrides.why_it_matters: Standard anti-waiver protection

overrides.override_summary: ND Securities Commissioner: certain franchise agreement provisions voided per ND Franchise Investment Law §51-19-09
overrides.why_it_matters: Multiple franchise agreement provisions may be unenforceable in ND

overrides.override_summary: Attorneys' fees provision applies ONLY if franchisor is the prevailing party
overrides.why_it_matters: One-directional attorneys' fees — franchisor cannot collect if licensee prevails

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire/acknowledgment can waive franchise law claims or disclaim reliance
overrides.why_it_matters: Standard anti-waiver protection
